description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player featuring a large, flared metal horn for sound amplification, attached to a curved, dark-colored arm. The base is a rectangular wooden box with a polished finish, supported by four short legs and adorned with subtle decorative details. On top of the wooden base sits a circular turntable platter, which holds a black vinyl record with a red label at its center. A metallic tonearm with a visible joint extends over the platter, poised to play the record. A hand-crank mechanism is attached to the side of the wooden base, indicating manual operation. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century phonographs, combining wood, metal, and plastic elements for both function and aesthetic appeal.
description: A vintage gramophone-style record player with a large metal horn, wooden base, and hand-crank mechanism. It features a circular turntable platter and a metallic tonearm poised over a vinyl record.
description_view_0: The record player is seen from the rear, highlighting the large flared horn and the curved arm extending from the back, with the hand-crank partially visible on the right side.
description_view_1: A side view showing the full length of the metal horn, the curved tonearm, and the vinyl record on the platter, with the hand-crank and base details clearly visible.
description_view_2: A front view directly facing the horn opening, with the turntable platter and record partially visible, and the hand-crank on the right side of the wooden base.
description_view_3: A side view from the opposite direction, showing the horn's profile, the tonearm over the record, and the hand-crank extending from the front right of the base.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present and circular, occupying a central position on the wooden base. It appears to be made of black plastic or metal, with a smooth, slightly glossy finish. The platter is large enough to hold a standard vinyl record, and a record with a red center label is currently placed on it. No visible grooves or patterns are apparent on the platter itself.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and gently curved, extending from the side of the base over the platter. It is medium in length, with a joint near its base for pivoting. The tonearm is positioned above the record, suggesting it is ready to play.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is not clearly visible, but its presence is implied at the end of the tonearm where it would typically contact the record.
